_doc: Field semantics registry (Consumer Compatibility Contract, §28.40). For every OPTIONAL rule-schema field that participates in a consumer's enforcement derivation, declares its `absent_default` (what a consumer reading OLD data — where the field is missing — MUST assume) and its `enforcement_relevant` flag (whether a consumer gate derives requirements from it). This makes a consumer's dual-read shim unambiguous: a field added on the CTP side is only safe if its absent-default is defined here, so legacy consumer state reads a defined answer instead of a new requirement.
_contract: CTP-side invariant: every NEW optional, enforcement-relevant rule field MUST be registered here with an absent_default in the same CL that adds it (audit-consumer-compatibility.sh enforces this). Consumers MUST treat a missing field as its absent_default and MUST NOT derive a new floor requirement from a field whose absent_default makes the rule inapplicable.

introduced_in.consumer_note: Missing => 'baseline' (pre-contract, grandfathered). Gate floors by epoch: enforce a floor only on tickets/content whose issue-epoch >= the rule's introduced_in epoch.

applies_to_prose.consumer_note: Missing => false. A rule is on the prose-projection floor ONLY when this is true; never derive a .md floor requirement from a rule where it is absent.

applies_to_prose_kinds.consumer_note: Only meaningful when applies_to_prose is true.

applies_to.consumer_note: Missing => the rule applies everywhere (no 4-axis restriction); routing falls back to the rule's own detector via enforced_by[0].

enforced_by.consumer_note: Missing => the rule is enforced solely by its in-repo detector (parity). A `required` tool that is absent HARD-FAILS; an optional/absent tool is not_enforced (advisory) — a consumer treating not_enforced as red must read this flag.

languages.consumer_note: Legacy v0.3 tags; superseded by applies_to. Not a floor input.

rule_state_history.consumer_note: Audit trail; not a floor input.
